Explanation: This report presents a well-structured analysis of chromatographic data with three sequential steps addressing baseline correction, cross-run alignment, and compound classification. Strengths include: (1) clear problem motivation addressing real sensor artifacts; (2) rigorous application of ALS baseline correction with appropriate peak detection parameters; (3) comprehensive gradient-based normalization across 11 runs; (4) appropriate use of multivariate outlier detection via Isolation Forest; and (5) sound statistical methodology using percentile-based thresholding. Writing quality is generally excellent with proper technical terminology. Visuals effectively support findings, though some figures (Figures 5-8 in appendix) appear redundant. The critical limitation is that the analysis reveals 98% mixed species classification and dominant 0% 'Conc B %' signal, suggesting potential experimental issues rather than successful compound profiling. The authors appropriately acknowledge these limitations and propose future directions. Minor areas for improvement: more discussion of parameter sensitivity and validation of the empirical thresholds would strengthen claims. Code is well-commented and includes error handling, demonstrating reproducibility.
